texto: Secrets are like plants. They can stay buried deep in the earth for a long time, but eventually they'll send up shoots and give themselves away. They have to. It's their nature. Just a tiny green stem at first. Which slowly, insidiously grows taller, stronger, unfolding itself, until there it is. A big fat secret, right in front of your face; a fully bloomed flower perfumed with the scent of deception.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Judy Reene Singer: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Secrets are like plants.
